title: sd update --status doesn't clear closedAt/closeReason when reopening
description:
## Symptom

Issues can end up with `status:"open"` co-existing with a `closedAt` timestamp and `closeReason`. They appear in `sd ready` (because status is open) but `sd show` displays a stale "closed at" timestamp and stale close reason.

Observed in overstory's `.seeds/issues.jsonl` on 2026-04-30 (overstory-4670, overstory-8e69 — both `status:"open"` with `closedAt` set and a triage-style close reason).

## Reproduction

```
sd close <id> --reason "..."
sd update <id> --status open
sd show <id>     # status: open, but closedAt and closeReason still set
```

## Root cause

`src/commands/update.ts:64-70` accepts `--status` changes but only writes the new status into the patch. It never clears `closedAt` / `closeReason` when the new status is non-`closed`, so stale close metadata persists.

```ts
if (typeof flags.status === "string") {
    const s = flags.status;
    if (!(VALID_STATUSES as readonly string[]).includes(s)) {
        throw new Error(`--status must be one of: ${VALID_STATUSES.join(", ")}`);
    }
    patch.status = s as Issue["status"];
}
```

## Fix

In `update.ts`, when `patch.status` is being set to something other than `"closed"`, also set `patch.closedAt = undefined` and `patch.closeReason = undefined`. (Same applies if a future `sd reopen` command is added — preferable, since reopen is a distinct verb.)

## Doctor follow-up

`src/commands/doctor.ts` has no check for status/closedAt consistency. Add `checkClosedFieldsConsistency`:

- `status !== "closed"` AND (`closedAt` set OR `closeReason` set) → fail (fixable: clear both fields)
- `status === "closed"` AND `closedAt` missing → warn (fixable: set to `updatedAt`)

Make it `--fix`-able so existing inconsistent records (like the two overstory issues) can be repaired.

## Acceptance

- After `sd close <id>` followed by `sd update <id> --status open`, `sd show <id>` reports no `closedAt` and no `closeReason`.
- `sd doctor` reports a failure on issues with mismatched status/closedAt; `sd doctor --fix` repairs them.
- Tests in `src/commands/update.test.ts` and `src/commands/doctor.test.ts` cover the close→reopen→show round-trip and the doctor check.
closeReason: Fixed in update.ts: --status to non-closed now clears closedAt/closeReason. Added doctor closed-fields-consistency check (fail on stale fields, warn on missing closedAt; both --fix-able). Tests added in update.test.ts and doctor.test.ts; doctor pass count bumped to 12.

title: sd plan: structured planning facilitation
description:
Extend seeds beyond issue tracking into a structured planning layer for AI agents. Plans are JSONL-stored, schema-validated, config-customizable, and produce child seeds with blocking edges wired automatically. Soft-coupled to mulch for prior-art enrichment.

Spec: PLAN_SPEC.md (root of seeds repo).

## Thesis
Most attention should go to planning, not review. Plans are the artifact reviewed; implementation should be straightforward enough that small models can execute. When implementation fails, fix the planning *process*, not the post-hoc code review.

## Architecture
- New file: .seeds/plans.jsonl (parallel to issues.jsonl, same locking model)
- Plans get their own ID space (pl-XXXX)
- Bidirectional linking: plan.seed ↔ issue.plan_id
- Templates declared in config.yaml plan_templates: block; AJV validates
- Children spawned 1:1 from steps[]; blocks wired via step indices

## Rollout (this epic blocks on phase features)
Phase 0: Validation foundation (AJV scaffold)
Phase 1: Core MVP — hardcoded feature template, basic spawn loop
Phase 2: Custom templates from config + lifecycle + --overwrite
Phase 3: Mulch prior_art enrichment + --record-decision
Phase 4: Sub-plans (nested decomposition)
Phase 5: Outcomes + review + bug/refactor templates + docs

## v1 surface (per planning alignment)
IN: phases 0-5, sub-plans, mulch enrichment, outcomes/review
OUT (followups): bug/refactor built-in templates land in phase 5 but are lower priority

## Decisions locked
- yaml.ts upgrade to nested (vs separate file) — keeps spec-as-written
- AJV as runtime dep (vs hand-rolled) — matches spec, prior art in mulch
- Shell out to ml query --json (soft coupling, language-agnostic)

title: Phase 0: Validation foundation (AJV)
description:
## Phase 0 — Validation foundation (AJV)

Establishes the validation primitives that all later `sd plan` phases consume. This phase ships the central `src/validation.ts` module and its tests, plus the `ajv` runtime dependency. It does NOT ship template-config-to-schema generation (Phase 2) — only hand-written schemas can be compiled by the primitives delivered here.

### Spec references

- `PLAN_SPEC.md:358–371` — Validation rules surface (required, min_length, min, step ref integrity, kind matching, template name resolution).
- `PLAN_SPEC.md:180–195` — Partial-state diff shape that validation failures must emit:
  ```json
  {
    "errors": [
      { "path": "sections.risks", "code": "required", "fix": "add a 'risks' array (string entries)" },
      { "path": "sections.steps", "code": "min", "fix": "add at least 1 more step entry" }
    ],
    "current": { "...plan-as-submitted..." }
  }
  ```

### Why this phase exists

`sd plan submit` and `sd plan validate` (later phases) both need a single, reusable validator that:

1. Compiles AJV schemas with consistent options (allErrors: true, strict mode, etc).
2. Translates AJV errors into the spec's stable error shape so the LLM can patch and resubmit without re-prompting.
3. Keeps the AJV import surface confined to one file, preserving the project's "minimal runtime dependencies" rule (CLAUDE.md — only chalk + commander today).

### Scope

In scope:
- Add `ajv` (^8.x) under `dependencies` in `package.json`.
- Create `src/validation.ts` exporting `compileSchema` and an error formatter.
- Define a TS interface for the partial-state diff structure.
- Unit tests in `src/validation.test.ts` covering valid/invalid cases.

Out of scope (later phases):
- Generating AJV schemas from `plan_templates` config (Phase 2 task 14).
- Wiring validation into `sd plan submit` / `sd plan validate` commands (Phase 1+).
- Mulch enrichment, prompt JSON, persistence — none of these touch this module.

### Tasks

- Task 0.1 — Add ajv runtime dep and scaffold `src/validation.ts` (this phase has a single task).

### Definition of done

- Phase 0 task is closed.
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all green.
- `ajv` appears in `dependencies`, not `devDependencies`.
- `src/validation.ts` is importable and consumed by zero callers yet (Phase 1 will wire it in).
closeReason: Phase 0 complete: ajv added as runtime dep, src/validation.ts ships compileSchema + formatErrors with PartialStateDiff/ErrorEntry types. 11 tests cover valid/invalid, required-path, minLength, minItems→min normalization, current round-trip, allErrors, fix non-empty, type mismatch. Quality gates green; ajv import boundary verified (only src/validation.ts imports it).

title: Add ajv runtime dep and scaffold src/validation.ts
description:
## Task 0.1 — Add ajv runtime dep and scaffold `src/validation.ts`

Part of Phase 0 (validation foundation). Establishes the single AJV-using module that every later `sd plan` command will consume.

### What

1. Add `ajv` (^8.x) to seeds runtime dependencies in `package.json` (under `dependencies`, NOT `devDependencies`). Run `bun install` to update lockfile.
2. Create `src/validation.ts` exporting at minimum:
   - `compileSchema(schema: object): ValidatorFn` — wraps `new Ajv({ allErrors: true, strict: true })` + `ajv.compile(schema)`. Returns a function `(data: unknown) => { valid: boolean, errors: ErrorEntry[] }` (or equivalent) that already runs the formatter under the hood, OR returns the raw compiled validator and exposes `formatErrors` separately. Pick whichever feels cleaner; document the choice in a top-of-file comment.
   - `formatErrors(ajvErrors, submitted): PartialStateDiff` — turns AJV's `ErrorObject[]` plus the originally-submitted plan into the spec's partial-state diff shape (see PLAN_SPEC.md:180–195).
   - A TypeScript `interface PartialStateDiff` (or similar name) describing `{ errors: ErrorEntry[]; current: unknown }` and `interface ErrorEntry { path: string; code: string; fix: string }`.
3. Mapping rules inside `formatErrors`:
   - AJV `instancePath` (e.g. `/sections/risks`) → spec `path` (e.g. `sections.risks`). Strip the leading `/`, replace remaining `/` with `.`. Empty string for root.
   - For `required` errors, append the missing property to the path (AJV reports `instancePath` as the parent and puts the missing key in `params.missingProperty`).
   - AJV `keyword` → spec `code` (`required`, `minLength`, `minItems`, `type`, etc.). For now do a 1:1 passthrough, but normalize `minItems` and `minLength` callers can choose to map to spec's shorter `min` if convenient — document the chosen convention.
   - Generate a human-readable `fix` string per error keyword. Examples:
     - `required` + missing `risks` → `"add a 'risks' array (string entries)"` style. Use the schema's surrounding context if available; otherwise emit a generic `"add a '<prop>' field"`.
     - `minItems` (got n, need m) → `"add at least <m-n> more entr<y|ies>"`.
     - `minLength` → `"expand '<path>' to at least <N> characters"`.
     - `type` mismatch → `"'<path>' must be a <expected-type>"`.
     - Fallback for unknown keywords: a string built from `keyword` + `params`.
4. `current` must round-trip the submitted object verbatim — pass it through unchanged (the LLM patches this and resubmits).

### Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:358–371, validation runs on every `sd plan submit` and on `sd plan validate <pl-id>`. The failure shape (PLAN_SPEC.md:180–195) is what lets the LLM patch-and-retry without re-running `sd plan prompt`. Centralising AJV in one module keeps the dep boundary tight: only `src/validation.ts` imports `ajv`, satisfying the project's "minimal runtime dependencies" rule (see seeds CLAUDE.md, "Dependencies" section).

### Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `package.json` lists `ajv` under `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`); `bun.lockb` (or equivalent lockfile) updated.
- [ ] `src/validation.ts` exists and exports `compileSchema`, `formatErrors`, and the `PartialStateDiff` / `ErrorEntry` types.
- [ ] Mapping is correct: AJV `instancePath` → spec `path` (slash-to-dot, leading slash stripped); `required` errors include the missing property in the path.
- [ ] `formatErrors` emits a non-empty `fix` string for every error (no empty/undefined fixes).
- [ ] `current` field round-trips the submitted object verbatim (deep-equal to input).
- [ ] `src/validation.test.ts` covers:
  - Valid object passes (no errors, `valid: true`).
  - Missing required field fails with `code: "required"` and the path includes the missing property.
  - Too-short string fails with `code: "minLength"` (or normalized equivalent — document choice).
  - Submitted object survives in `current` byte-for-byte.
  - Multiple simultaneous errors all surface (AJV `allErrors: true` is honored).
- [ ] `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all pass.
- [ ] No file outside `src/validation.ts` imports `ajv` (grep check).

### Out of scope

- Generating AJV schemas from `plan_templates:` YAML config — that is Phase 2 task 14. Phase 0 only ships hand-written schema support.
- Wiring this validator into any `sd plan ...` subcommand — Phase 1+.
- Mulch enrichment, prompt JSON shape, plan persistence — unrelated subsystems.

### Files

- `src/validation.ts` (new)
- `src/validation.test.ts` (new)
- `package.json` (edit — add ajv)
- `bun.lock` / `bun.lockb` (edit — regenerate)

### Implementation hints

- Tests follow seeds convention: real I/O where needed, no mocks. Validation is pure so just feed JS objects — no temp dirs required for this module.
- Keep the file small (<150 LOC); the hard work is in keyword-to-`fix` translation. A `switch (keyword)` inside `formatErrors` is fine.
- If AJV's strict mode complains about meta-keywords used in tests, add only the necessary `strict: false` overrides locally inside tests; production `compileSchema` should stay strict.
closeReason: Phase 0 complete: ajv added as runtime dep, src/validation.ts ships compileSchema + formatErrors with PartialStateDiff/ErrorEntry types. 11 tests cover valid/invalid, required-path, minLength, minItems→min normalization, current round-trip, allErrors, fix non-empty, type mismatch. Quality gates green; ajv import boundary verified (only src/validation.ts imports it).

title: Phase 1: Core MVP (sd plan command tree, hardcoded feature template)
description:
## What

Phase 1 of the `sd plan` feature (parent epic seeds-8fdb). Ships a working `sd plan` command tree with a single hardcoded `feature` template, basic spawn loop, and integration with `sd ready` / `sd show` / `sd list`.

Spec: PLAN_SPEC.md
- Architecture & on-disk format: lines 22-107
- Lifecycle + command surface: lines 109-155
- Walkthrough protocol + JSON shapes: lines 159-256
- Default feature template: lines 313-325

## Why

This is the MVP slice — proves the architecture end-to-end (types, store, template+schema, command parent, prompt/submit/show/list/validate, integration with existing commands) without taking on mulch enrichment, sub-plans, or YAML-configurable templates. Those are Phase 2/3.

## Scope (constituent tasks)

1. Plan type + Issue field extensions in `src/types.ts`
2. Plan store layer in `src/store.ts`
3. Hardcoded built-in `feature` template + AJV schema (`src/plan-templates/feature.ts`)
4. `sd plan` command parent + `sd plan templates` subcommand
5. `sd plan prompt <seed-id>` command
6. `sd plan submit <seed-id> --plan <file>` command
7. `sd plan show <pl-id>` command
8. `sd plan list` command with filters
9. `sd plan validate <pl-id>` command
10. `sd ready`/`sd show`/`sd list` plan-awareness integration

## Out of scope (Phase 1)

- Mulch enrichment (`prior_art` arrays remain empty)
- Sub-plans / nested plans / `requires_plan`
- Custom templates from `.seeds/config.yaml` (YAML upgrade is Phase 2)
- `sd plan outcome`, `sd plan review`, `--overwrite` behavior (Phase 2/3)
- `--record-decision` outbound mulch write

## Constraints

- `feature` template is a hardcoded TypeScript object, not loaded from YAML
- Validation uses a hand-written AJV schema via the `compileSchema` primitive from Phase 0's `src/validation.ts`
- Phase 1 must NOT depend on Phase 2's yaml.ts upgrade
- Existing seeds remain valid (additive `plan_id` / `plan_step_index` fields)

## Acceptance

- All 10 child tasks closed
- `sd plan prompt` → LLM-fillable JSON → `sd plan submit` → spawned children with correct `blockedBy` wiring works end-to-end
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` all green
closeReason: Phase 1 MVP complete: all 10 child tasks closed; bun test/lint/typecheck green (366 tests)

title: Plan type + Issue field extensions in src/types.ts
description:
## What

Add a new `Plan` interface and extend the existing `Issue` interface with optional planning back-pointers in `src/types.ts`.

PLAN_SPEC.md references:
- Plan row shape: lines 92-98
- Issue field additions: lines 100-107

### `Plan` interface

```ts
interface Plan {
  id: string;                      // "pl-{4hex}"
  seed: string;                    // parent seed id
  template: string;                // e.g. "feature"
  status: "draft" | "approved" | "active" | "done";
  revision: number;
  sections: Record<string, unknown>;
  children: string[];              // spawned child seed ids
  outcome?: "success" | "partial" | "failure";
  outcomeNote?: string;
  reviewedBy?: string;
  createdAt: string;               // ISO 8601
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### `Issue` extension

Two new optional fields (additive; backward-compatible):

- `plan_id?: string` — the plan this seed is part of (parent or spawned child).
- `plan_step_index?: number` — for spawned children, index in the plan's `steps[]` array (enables stale-child detection on revision).

### Constants

Add `PLANS_FILE = "plans.jsonl"` next to existing `ISSUES_FILE` / `TEMPLATES_FILE`.

## Why

Foundation type for every other Phase 1 task. Has to land before store, template, or commands can be built. Keeping the Issue extension additive guarantees existing JSONL rows remain valid.

## Acceptance

- `bun run typecheck` passes
- All existing tests still pass (`bun test`)
- `Plan` and `PLANS_FILE` are exported from `src/types.ts`
- A `Plan` literal with all required fields type-checks; one with extras (`outcome`, `reviewedBy`) also type-checks
- Existing `Issue` rows in fixtures still parse without the new fields

## Files

- `src/types.ts` (edit)
closeReason: Plan + Issue field extensions added in src/types.ts; typecheck and tests pass

title: Plan store layer in src/store.ts
description:
## What

Add a plan storage layer to `src/store.ts` mirroring the existing issue/template helpers.

New exports:
- `plansPath(root: string): string` — resolve `.seeds/plans.jsonl`
- `readPlans(root: string): Promise<Plan[]>` — read with dedup-on-read (last occurrence wins, mirrors issue dedup)
- `writePlans(root: string, plans: Plan[]): Promise<void>` — atomic temp+rename under `withLock`
- `appendPlan(root: string, plan: Plan): Promise<void>` — locked append

Use the same lock model as issues:
- `withLock` wrapper
- `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` lock files
- 30s stale, 50ms retry, 5s timeout
- Atomic writes via temp file + rename for full rewrites

Reference PLAN_SPEC.md lines 92-98 for the plan row shape and CLAUDE.md "Concurrency" section for the lock contract.

## Why

Every plan command (`prompt`, `submit`, `show`, `list`, `validate`) goes through this layer. Reusing the existing patterns guarantees the same multi-agent safety properties seeds already provides for issues.

## Acceptance

- New functions exported from `src/store.ts`
- Tests in `src/store.test.ts` cover:
  - Round-trip: write → read returns the same plans
  - Dedup-on-read: duplicate plan IDs in the JSONL collapse to last-occurrence
  - Append safety: concurrent `appendPlan` calls do not corrupt the file
  - Atomic rewrite: a `writePlans` call interrupted partway leaves the original file intact (or at least leaves no partial line)
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` green

## Files

- `src/store.ts` (edit)
- `src/store.test.ts` (edit — add plan tests)

## Depends on

Task 1 (Plan type must exist before the store can read/write it).

title: Phase 2: Custom templates from config + lifecycle + --overwrite
description:
## What

Phase 2 of the `sd plan` feature (parent epic: seeds-8fdb). Replaces Phase 1's hardcoded `feature` template with user-defined plan templates declared in `.seeds/config.yaml`, adds plan lifecycle auto-transitions (draft → approved → active → done), and implements `--overwrite` semantics for plan revisions.

Phase 2 covers:
1. Upgrade YAML parser to support nested maps + lists (required for `plan_templates:` block).
2. Parse `plan_templates:` config block into typed `PlanTemplate` records.
3. Generate AJV schemas dynamically from `PlanTemplate` config (replaces Phase 1 hand-written feature schema).
4. Wire plan lifecycle auto-transitions on child seed status changes.
5. Implement `sd plan submit --overwrite` with revision increment + obsolete-child flagging.

## Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:11–19 (Design Principles), plans must be customizable per-project. Hardcoded templates only cover the default `feature` shape; teams need `spike`, `bug`, `refactor`, and bespoke templates without forking seeds. The lifecycle transitions and `--overwrite` semantics are explicit requirements in PLAN_SPEC.md:109–127 (lifecycle) and 374–391 (re-submission).

This phase transforms `sd plan` from a hardcoded prototype into a config-driven planning layer.

## Acceptance

- Plan templates declared in `.seeds/config.yaml` under `plan_templates:` are loadable, validatable, and overridable.
- Built-in `feature` template behaves identically to Phase 1 when no config override exists.
- Plan status auto-transitions: `draft → approved` (on submit), `approved → active` (on first child `in_progress`), `active → done` (when all children closed); reverts `done → active` if a closed child reopens.
- `sd plan submit --overwrite` rewrites `plans.jsonl` row, increments `revision`, flags obsolete children to stderr (does NOT auto-close), spawns new children for new steps.
- Re-submitting without `--overwrite` is rejected when a non-`draft` plan exists for the seed.
- All 5 sub-tasks closed; phase blocking edges cleared.

## Files

- `src/yaml.ts` (extend or replace)
- `src/types.ts` (add `PlanTemplate`, `SectionSpec`)
- `src/config.ts` (add `loadPlanTemplates`)
- `src/plan-schema.ts` (NEW — generates AJV schemas from templates)
- `src/commands/plan-submit.ts` (extend with `--overwrite`)
- `src/commands/update.ts`, `src/commands/close.ts` (lifecycle hooks)

## Spec references

- PLAN_SPEC.md:36–78 — config.yaml additions, plan_templates structure
- PLAN_SPEC.md:109–127 — lifecycle state machine
- PLAN_SPEC.md:261–325 — templates customization, kinds, default feature template
- PLAN_SPEC.md:374–391 — re-submission and --overwrite semantics

## Cross-phase

Depends on Phase 1 landing (plan command tree, store, types). The orchestrator wires Phase 1 → Phase 2 blocking at the phase-feature level.
closeReason: Phase 2 complete: yaml parser nesting, plan_templates loader, AJV schema gen, lifecycle auto-transitions, --overwrite. 428 tests pass. All 5 sub-tasks closed.

title: Hardcoded built-in feature template + AJV schema
description:
## What

Create `src/plan-templates/feature.ts` (or similar location) that exports the built-in `feature` template as a hardcoded TypeScript object plus a hand-written AJV schema for validating submissions.

### Six default sections

Per PLAN_SPEC.md lines 313-325 and 38-78:

1. `context` — required, `kind: text`, `min_length: 50`
2. `approach` — required, `kind: text`
3. `alternatives` — optional, `kind: list`, item is `{name: text, rejected_because: text}`
4. `steps` — required, `kind: steps`, `min: 2`. Each step is `{title, type, priority, blocks: number[]}`. (Phase 1 ignores `plan_template` on steps — that's nested-plans, Phase 3.)
5. `risks` — optional, `kind: list`, item `text` (Phase 1: no mulch enrichment, but the `mulch_source: failure` hint can live in the template metadata for forward-compat)
6. `acceptance` — required, `kind: list`, item `text`, `min: 1`

### AJV schema

Hand-write the JSON schema. Use `compileSchema` from Phase 0's `src/validation.ts` (already in place). The schema must enforce:
- Required sections present and non-empty
- `min_length` on `context`
- `min` on `steps` and `acceptance`
- `steps[].blocks` references valid step indices (no forward references beyond array length, no self-reference) — this is the structural validation called out in PLAN_SPEC.md lines 366-369
- `alternatives[]` items match the `{name, rejected_because}` shape

### Partial-state diff shape

On validation failure, callers must be able to emit the partial-state JSON in PLAN_SPEC.md lines 180-195:

```json
{
  "errors": [{ "path": "sections.X", "code": "...", "fix": "..." }],
  "current": { ...plan-as-submitted... }
}
```

Provide a helper that converts AJV errors into this shape (or returns enough information for the submit command to do so).

## Why

This is the validation core. `sd plan prompt` reads the template metadata to emit the prompt JSON; `sd plan submit` and `sd plan validate` use the schema to gate writes. Hardcoding it (vs. deriving from YAML) lets Phase 1 ship without depending on Phase 2's yaml.ts upgrade.

## Acceptance

- A valid plan submission (all required sections, >=2 steps with valid `blocks` indices, >=1 acceptance, context >= 50 chars) passes
- Each of the following fails with the documented partial-state diff shape:
  - Missing required section (e.g. omit `context`)
  - `context` too short (< 50 chars)
  - `steps` with only 1 entry
  - `steps[0].blocks: [0]` (self-reference)
  - `steps[0].blocks: [5]` when there are only 2 steps (out of range)
  - `acceptance: []`
- Tests for each of the above in a new `src/plan-templates/feature.test.ts`
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` green

## Files

- `src/plan-templates/feature.ts` (new)
- `src/plan-templates/feature.test.ts` (new)
- May need a small `src/plan-templates/index.ts` registry that maps name → template (Phase 1 has only `feature`)

## Depends on

- Task 1 (types)
- Phase 0's `src/validation.ts` (`compileSchema`)
closeReason: feature template + AJV schema in src/plan-templates/feature.ts; structural blocks-index check via second pass; 10 tests covering golden + 6 documented failure cases

title: Upgrade src/yaml.ts to support nested maps + lists
description:
## What

Upgrade `src/yaml.ts` from the current flat key-value parser (~50 LOC) to support nested maps and lists. This is required to parse the `plan_templates:` block in `.seeds/config.yaml`.

Two acceptable approaches — implementer's call:
1. Extend the in-tree parser (preserves zero-runtime-dep posture; matches CLAUDE.md "minimal runtime dependencies" convention).
2. Add `js-yaml` (~9KB) as a runtime dep.

Document the choice in the commit message.

## Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:36–78, the config.yaml block needed for Phase 2 is deeply nested:

```yaml
plan_templates:
  feature:
    sections:
      context:
        required: true
        kind: text
        min_length: 50
        prompt: "..."
      alternatives:
        kind: list
        item:
          name: { kind: text }
          rejected_because: { kind: text }
      steps:
        kind: steps
        min: 2
```

The current parser cannot represent this. Without nested-map + list support, no other Phase 2 task can begin.

## Acceptance

- Parses the `plan_templates:` block from PLAN_SPEC.md:42–77 round-trip into a typed structure.
- Existing flat-config tests in `src/yaml.test.ts` still pass unchanged.
- New tests cover:
  - Nested maps (3+ levels deep).
  - Lists of strings (e.g., `[a, b, c]` and block-style `- a\n- b`).
  - Lists of objects.
  - Mixed quoted (`"foo: bar"`) and unquoted scalars.
  - Inline flow maps (e.g., `{ kind: text }`) since spec uses them.
  - Booleans, integers, and string scalars correctly typed.
- `bun test src/yaml.test.ts && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean.

## Files

- `src/yaml.ts` — parser implementation
- `src/yaml.test.ts` — extend tests
- `package.json` — add `js-yaml` if that route is chosen

## Spec references

- PLAN_SPEC.md:36–78 — config.yaml additions

## Notes

Document parser choice in the commit message body. If extending in-tree, keep the LOC budget reasonable; if it grows past ~200 LOC, the dep route is probably the better call.
closeReason: yaml parser upgraded: nested maps, block sequences, flow maps/seqs, typed scalars; in-tree (no js-yaml dep); 35 tests

title: Phase 3: Mulch prior_art enrichment + --record-decision
description:
# Phase 3: Mulch prior_art enrichment + --record-decision

Phase 3 of the `sd plan` rollout. Makes `sd plan prompt` smarter by injecting
prior art from mulch (when `ml` is on PATH) into each section's `prior_art`
array, and adds an opt-in outbound `ml record` write on `sd plan submit`.

## What

Three pieces of work:
1. Domain inference helper used by both prompt enrichment and outbound write
   (PLAN_SPEC.md:347).
2. Per-section prior_art population via shelling out to `ml query --json`
   (PLAN_SPEC.md:344-352, prompt JSON shape PLAN_SPEC.md:200-234, section hint
   PLAN_SPEC.md:36-78).
3. `sd plan submit --record-decision` outbound write — best-effort, off by
   default (PLAN_SPEC.md:354-356).

## Why

- Plans grounded in prior conventions, patterns, decisions, and failures are
  better plans. Mulch already accumulates that prior art; surfacing it in the
  planning prompt closes the loop.
- Soft coupling preserves seeds' standalone story (PLAN_SPEC.md:17) — `ml`
  absent must not break planning.

## Acceptance

- `sd plan prompt <seed-id>` populates `prior_art` arrays on sections when
  `ml` is on PATH and a domain can be inferred; arrays are empty otherwise
  with no error.
- `sd plan submit --record-decision` invokes `ml record` on success when `ml`
  is available; submit succeeds atomically even when the outbound `ml` call
  fails.
- All 3 child tasks closed with passing tests.

## Design decision (locked)

Shell out to `ml query --json` / `ml record` via `Bun.spawnSync` and resolve
`ml` with `Bun.which("ml")`. **Do NOT** import `@os-eco/mulch-cli`
programmatically — soft coupling and language-agnostic.

## Dependencies

- Depends on Phase 1 (prompt + submit commands must exist).
- Wired at the feature level by the orchestrator.

title: Parse plan_templates block from config.yaml into typed PlanTemplate
description:
## What

Add typed loading of the `plan_templates:` block from `.seeds/config.yaml` into seeds.

1. Add to `src/types.ts`:
   ```ts
   export interface SectionSpec {
     required: boolean;
     kind: "text" | "list" | "steps" | Record<string, SectionSpec>;
     prompt: string;
     min_length?: number;
     min?: number;
     item?: SectionSpec | "text";
     mulch_source?: string;
   }
   export interface PlanTemplate {
     name: string;
     sections: Record<string, SectionSpec>;
   }
   ```
2. Extend `src/config.ts` with `loadPlanTemplates(): Record<string, PlanTemplate>`.
   - Read parsed config (relies on the upgraded parser from the blocking task).
   - If `plan_templates:` block is missing or empty, return built-ins (just `feature` from Phase 1 — keep behavior backward-compatible).
   - User-declared templates with the same name as a built-in override the built-in.
   - Validate every section has `required` (bool), `kind` (known value or object spec), and `prompt` (string).
   - Reject unknown `kind` values with a clear error: `plan_templates.<name>.sections.<section>: unknown kind '<value>' (expected text|list|steps|object)`.

## Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:261–325, templates are config-declared with required/optional sections. Phase 2 needs typed access to these templates so the schema generator (next task) can compile them into AJV validators.

## Acceptance

- `loadPlanTemplates()` returns typed `PlanTemplate` records.
- Built-in `feature` template is loaded when config has no `plan_templates:` block.
- User-declared `feature` template fully overrides the built-in.
- Custom templates (e.g., `spike` from PLAN_SPEC.md:285–310) load correctly.
- Unknown `kind` is rejected with the error format above.
- Round-trip test: read a YAML file with a custom template, re-serialize via the typed structure, parse again — equivalent.
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean.

## Files

- `src/types.ts` — add `PlanTemplate`, `SectionSpec` (and re-export)
- `src/config.ts` — add `loadPlanTemplates`
- `src/config.test.ts` (or new `src/plan-templates.test.ts`) — round-trip + validation tests

## Spec references

- PLAN_SPEC.md:36–78 — config.yaml plan_templates block
- PLAN_SPEC.md:80–90 — section field reference
- PLAN_SPEC.md:261–325 — templates customization, kinds, default feature template

## Blocked by

- Task: "Upgrade src/yaml.ts to support nested maps + lists"
closeReason: added SectionSpec/PlanTemplate to types.ts; loadPlanTemplates() in config.ts with built-in fallback + override semantics; renamed runtime PlanTemplate to CompiledTemplate to avoid collision; 9 tests

title: sd plan prompt <seed-id> [--template <name>]
description:
## What

Implement `sd plan prompt <seed-id> [--template <name>]` — emits the structured prompt JSON the LLM consumes to fill out a plan.

PLAN_SPEC.md references:
- Successful path: lines 162-175
- Prompt JSON shape: lines 200-234
- Default template inference: line 271 + Open Question 4 (line 429)

### Default template inference

Per the spec recommendation at line 429:
- `task` → `feature`
- `bug` → `bug`  *(deferred — not in Phase 1)*
- `feature` → `feature`
- `epic` → `feature`

In Phase 1 only `feature` exists, so any seed type maps to `feature` by default. `--template` always overrides.

### Output shape

Emit JSON matching PLAN_SPEC.md lines 200-234:

```json
{
  "plan_request": {
    "seed": "seeds-9c4d",
    "template": "feature",
    "instructions": "Fill every section. Required fields are marked.",
    "sections": [
      { "name": "context", "required": true, "kind": "text", "min_length": 50, "prompt": "...", "prior_art": [] },
      ...
    ],
    "validation": {
      "all_required_present": true,
      "min_steps": 2,
      "min_acceptance": 1
    }
  }
}
```

`prior_art: []` — Phase 1 emits empty arrays. Phase 3 fills them from mulch.

### Validation rules block

`min_steps` and `min_acceptance` are computed from the template's section `min` values. `all_required_present` is always `true` (it's an instruction, not a config knob).

### Errors

- Unknown seed → exit non-zero with clear message
- Unknown template name → exit non-zero, hint the available list

## Why

Stage 1 of the planning loop. The output is the contract between seeds and the LLM; getting the JSON shape exactly right means the LLM can fill it out and round-trip back through `submit` without extra glue.

## Acceptance

- `sd plan prompt seeds-XXXX` (human, non-JSON) prints something readable
- `sd plan prompt seeds-XXXX --json` emits the full `plan_request` JSON above
- Default template is inferred from seed.type (`task`/`feature`/`epic` → `feature`)
- `--template feature` is accepted; `--template missing` errors with the available list
- Unknown seed errors cleanly
- Tests in `src/commands/plan.test.ts` cover human output, JSON output, default inference, and both error paths
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` green

## Files

- `src/commands/plan.ts` (edit — add `prompt` subcommand)
- `src/commands/plan.test.ts` (edit)

## Depends on

- Task 1 (types)
- Task 2 (store — read seed)
- Task 3 (template definition + section metadata)
- Task 4 (command parent)
closeReason: sd plan prompt landed: --json emits documented plan_request shape, --template override + missing-template error, unknown-seed error, default inference all tested

title: Domain inference helper for sd plan (mulch integration)
description:
# Domain inference helper for `sd plan`

Shared helper that infers the mulch `--domain` to query for a given seed.
Used by both prior_art enrichment (`sd plan prompt`) and outbound
`--record-decision` (`sd plan submit`).

## What

Implement a helper that, given a seed, returns the mulch domain to use (or
`null` if none can be inferred). Inference order per PLAN_SPEC.md:347:

1. Explicit `--domain <name>` flag passed on the command.
2. Seed's `labels` intersected with mulch's declared domains
   (`ml status --json` lists them).
3. File anchors: `git diff --name-only` against any files referenced in the
   seed's description, mapped to mulch domains by directory anchor.

If none match, return `null` and callers skip prior_art queries (empty
arrays) — does not error.

## Why

- Both Phase 3 features (per-section `prior_art` enrichment and
  `--record-decision` outbound write) need the same domain answer.
  Centralizing keeps behavior consistent and testable.
- Spec PLAN_SPEC.md:344-352 mandates soft coupling: when `ml status` errors
  or returns no domains, planning still works.

## Acceptance

- Returns the explicit `--domain` value when passed, with no `ml` call.
- Returns the matching domain when seed labels intersect `ml status --json`
  domains.
- Falls back to file-anchor mapping via `git diff --name-only` against files
  parsed out of the seed description.
- Returns `null` (graceful degradation) when:
  - `ml` is not on PATH (`Bun.which("ml")` returns null), OR
  - `ml status --json` exits non-zero, OR
  - no signal matches.
- Unit tests cover each branch including the degradation paths. Tests use a
  fake `ml` script on PATH (a tempdir bin/ prepended to `$PATH`) to avoid
  depending on a real mulch install.

## Files

- New: `src/plan-domain.ts` (helper module).
- New: `src/plan-domain.test.ts` (colocated tests, real I/O per project
  testing convention).
- Call site: `src/commands/plan.ts` `prompt` handler (passes inferred
  domain into per-section enrichment).

## Implementation notes

- Use `Bun.which("ml")` to resolve `ml` — explicitly required; do not assume
  PATH.
- Use `Bun.spawnSync` for shelling out (matches existing seeds pattern for
  git invocations).
- File-anchor parsing: simple regex over the description for tokens that
  look like paths (`src/...`, `*.ts`, etc.), then `git diff --name-only`
  filtered to those references.
- This task **blocks** the per-section prior_art task and the
  `--record-decision` task. Both consume the same inferred domain.

title: Phase 4: Sub-plans (nested decomposition)
description:
## What

Phase 4 unlocks recursive plan decomposition. A step in a plan template's `steps` kind may declare `plan_template: <name>`; on `sd plan submit`, the spawned child seed is created with `requires_plan: true`. `sd plan show` then recurses through nested plans up to a configurable depth (default 3).

Spec references:
- PLAN_SPEC.md:328-342 (Nested Plans, including the load-bearing invariant at line 342: "A child seed marked `requires_plan: true` is excluded from `sd ready` until its sub-plan reaches `approved`.")
- PLAN_SPEC.md:36-78 (steps kind on plan templates — host for `plan_template` field)
- PLAN_SPEC.md:425, 430 (open question on `max_plan_depth` — adopt spec recommendation: configurable in config.yaml, default 3)

## Why

Without nested plans, epics that decompose into other epics force a context switch: plan the parent, manually file child epics, plan each separately. With sub-plans, a single plan submission can declare which children themselves require planning, and the LLM walks each via `sd plan prompt <child>` recursively. This decomposes complex work in-place without losing the plan-graph topology.

The `requires_plan` invariant (spec:342) guarantees no implementation begins on un-planned epic branches — a critical safety property for autonomous swarms.

## Acceptance

- A step with `plan_template: <name>` spawns a child seed where `requires_plan: true`.
- A seed with `requires_plan: true` is hidden from `sd ready` until its plan reaches `approved`.
- `sd plan show` recursively renders nested plans down to `max_plan_depth` (default 3), with truncation hints at the boundary.
- Sub-plans can stack arbitrarily deep at the data layer; only display is depth-limited.
- All three child tasks closed; quality gates pass.

## Tasks

1. `requires_plan` field on Issue + `sd ready` exclusion
2. Step `plan_template` field — spawn child with `requires_plan: true`
3. `sd plan show` recursive display + `max_plan_depth` config

## Dependencies

- Depends on Phase 1 (plan data model) and Phase 2 (custom templates — `plan_template` field is part of template config). The orchestrator wires Phase 2 -> Phase 4 at the feature level.

## Files (cross-task)

- `src/types.ts` — `Issue.requires_plan`, `PlanTemplate.steps[].plan_template`
- `src/commands/plan.ts` — submit (spawn with requires_plan), show (recursive)
- `src/commands/ready.ts`, `src/filter.ts` — exclude requires_plan seeds
- `src/config.ts`, `.seeds/config.yaml` — `max_plan_depth`
closeReason: Phase 4 complete: requires_plan + plan_template + recursive show shipped; tests cover all acceptance criteria; quality gates green

title: AJV schema generation from PlanTemplate config
description:
## What

Replace Phase 1's hand-written feature plan schema with a `generatePlanSchema(template: PlanTemplate): JSONSchema` function that walks `SectionSpec` recursively and emits a JSON Schema honoring all declared validation rules.

Add a new module `src/plan-schema.ts` (or similar). Use the validation primitives from `src/validation.ts` (added in Phase 0).

Schema generation must honor:
- `required` (top-level array of section names where `required: true`).
- `kind: text` → `{ type: "string", minLength: <min_length || 1> }`.
- `kind: list` → `{ type: "array", minItems: <min || 0>, items: <item-schema> }`.
- `kind: steps` → `{ type: "array", minItems: <min || 0>, items: <step-schema> }` where step-schema is `{title, type, priority, blocks, plan_template?}`.
- `item: "text"` → `{ type: "string" }`.
- `item: <object spec>` → `{ type: "object", properties: ..., required: ... }` recursively.
- Object-spec `kind` (nested `Record<string, SectionSpec>`) → recursive object schema.

## Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:312, "Validation is generated from the template config the same way mulch generates AJV schemas from `custom_types`." Phase 1 shipped a hand-written feature schema as a stopgap; Phase 2 must dynamically generate schemas from any template, including user-defined ones.

Without dynamic schema generation, custom templates can't be validated, defeating the customization goal of Phase 2.

## Acceptance

- `generatePlanSchema(template)` returns a JSON Schema object compatible with the validation harness from `src/validation.ts`.
- Round-trip test: built-in `feature` template produces a schema that validates the same submissions accepted by Phase 1's hand-written schema (and rejects the same failures).
- Custom template test: the `spike` example from PLAN_SPEC.md:285–310 compiles to a valid schema and validates submissions correctly.
- Edge cases tested:
  - Required vs optional section presence.
  - `min_length` enforcement on text.
  - `min` enforcement on list and steps.
  - Object-spec list items (e.g., `alternatives` in feature template).
  - Step `blocks` array references — values must be integers (deeper validation that they reference valid step indices is fine to keep at submit-time runtime checks rather than schema-level).
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean.

## Files

- `src/plan-schema.ts` (NEW) — `generatePlanSchema` + helpers
- `src/plan-schema.test.ts` (NEW)
- `src/commands/plan-submit.ts` — swap the hand-written schema call site for `generatePlanSchema(loadPlanTemplates()[name])`

## Spec references

- PLAN_SPEC.md:80–90 — section field reference (validation surface)
- PLAN_SPEC.md:273–280 — section kind reference
- PLAN_SPEC.md:285–310 — custom template example (spike)
- PLAN_SPEC.md:358–371 — validation requirements

## Blocked by

- Task: "Parse plan_templates block from config.yaml into typed PlanTemplate"
closeReason: src/plan-schema.ts: generatePlanSchema + compilePlanTemplate; replaces hand-written feature schema; plan.ts uses loadPlanTemplates()+compilePlanTemplate; deleted obsolete plan-templates/; 16 tests

title: Per-section prior_art via ml query --json shell-out
description:
# Per-section prior_art via `ml query --json` shell-out

For each section on a plan template, populate the section's `prior_art`
array with top mulch records of the appropriate type when `ml` is available.

## What

In the `sd plan prompt` flow, for each section, resolve the mulch record
type to query:

- Explicit `mulch_source: <type>` hint on the section (PLAN_SPEC.md:36-78,
  see `risks` example at line 70 with `mulch_source: failure`).
- Otherwise, well-known section name defaults (PLAN_SPEC.md:349):
  - `approach` -> pattern AND decision
  - `risks`   -> failure
  - `acceptance` -> guide
- Sections without a hint and not matching a well-known name -> skip.

For each (section, type) pair, shell out:

```
ml query --domain <inferred-domain> --type <type> --json --limit 5
```

Top results become `prior_art` entries with the shape from
PLAN_SPEC.md:222-225:

```
[{ "id": "mx-902", "type": "failure", "summary": "...", "relevance": <number> }]
```

## Why

PLAN_SPEC.md:344-352: prior art grounds the LLM's planning in real
conventions, patterns, decisions, and failures already recorded in mulch.
Soft coupling means absent `ml` must not break the prompt.

## Acceptance

- Sections with `mulch_source` hints get `prior_art` populated when `ml` is
  available and a domain is inferred.
- Well-known section names (`approach`, `risks`, `acceptance`) get the
  correct default record types per PLAN_SPEC.md:349.
- `prior_art` entry shape matches PLAN_SPEC.md:222-225
  (`{id, type, summary, relevance}`).
- **Integration test 1:** stub `ml` via a fake script on PATH (tempdir bin/
  prepended to `$PATH`) that emits canned JSON. Assert `prior_art` is
  populated correctly across multiple sections.
- **Integration test 2:** `ml` absent (`Bun.which("ml")` returns null) ->
  all `prior_art` arrays are empty, no crash, no error logged to stderr,
  validation rules unaffected, prompt JSON still emitted.
- **Integration test 3:** `ml query` exits non-zero for one section -> that
  section's `prior_art` is empty; other sections still populated; submit
  flow unaffected.
- No domain inferred (helper returns null) -> all `prior_art` arrays empty.

## Files

- New: `src/plan-mulch.ts` (helper module that wraps `ml query` shell-out).
- New: `src/plan-mulch.test.ts` (colocated integration tests).
- Call site: `src/commands/plan.ts` `prompt` handler — after building the
  prompt JSON skeleton, walks sections and fills `prior_art` arrays.

## Implementation notes

- Use `Bun.spawnSync` (already used elsewhere in seeds for git).
- Resolve `ml` via `Bun.which(\"ml\")` — explicitly required, easy to forget.
- Graceful degradation: any failure in the mulch path produces empty
  `prior_art`, never throws, never logs to stderr (silent fall-through).
- Reuses the domain inference helper from the blocker task.

title: sd plan submit <seed-id> --plan <file>
description:
## What

Implement `sd plan submit <seed-id> --plan <file>` — the validation + spawn loop.

PLAN_SPEC.md references:
- Successful flow: lines 162-175 (output shape) + 236-257 (submission JSON shape)
- Validation failure flow: lines 178-197
- Re-submission rejection: lines 374-391 (Phase 1 implements rejection only; `--overwrite` is Phase 2)
- `blocks: [step_index]` translation: line 257

### Behavior

1. Read plan JSON from `<file>` or `-` (stdin).
2. Validate against the template's AJV schema (Task 3).
3. **On validation failure**: emit the partial-state diff JSON to stderr (PLAN_SPEC.md lines 180-195 shape), exit non-zero. Do not write anything.
4. **On success**:
   - Generate a new plan id `pl-{4hex}` (mirror seed id generator from `src/id.ts`)
   - For each entry in `sections.steps[]`, allocate a new seed id and create a child seed: title/type/priority from the step, plus `plan_id` and `plan_step_index` set on the child
   - Translate `step.blocks: [index]` references → child seed ids → write to each child's `blockedBy` field
   - Append the plan row to `plans.jsonl` with `status: approved`, `revision: 1`, `children: [...]`
   - Update the parent seed: set `plan_id` back-pointer
5. Resubmission of a non-`draft` plan for the same seed → reject with a clear message ("Use --overwrite to replace it" — but Phase 1 does not implement `--overwrite`; just print the rejection and exit non-zero)

### Spawn ordering / atomicity

All writes (children + plan row + parent seed update) must happen under appropriate locks so a crash mid-spawn doesn't leave dangling state. Acceptable approach: lock issues + plans, build all rows in memory, then commit. Document the chosen ordering in code comments.

### Output

- Human: matches PLAN_SPEC.md lines 172-175 (`✓ plan pl-a1b2 created`, `✓ spawned N child seeds: ...`, `✓ <parent> now blocked by N children`)
- `--json`: structured `{plan_id, children: [...], parent_seed}`

## Why

This is the engine of the planning workflow. Getting `blocks: [step_index]` → `blockedBy` translation right is the linchpin — the index-to-id remap has to happen *after* all child IDs are allocated.

## Acceptance

- **Golden path test**: 4-step plan with `steps[1].blocks: [0]` and `steps[3].blocks: [2]` produces:
  - 4 child seeds in `issues.jsonl`
  - Each child has `plan_id` and `plan_step_index` set
  - `seeds-aa01.blockedBy === []`, `seeds-aa02.blockedBy === [seeds-aa01]`, etc. (correct id remap)
  - Parent seed gains `plan_id` back-pointer
  - One row in `plans.jsonl` with `status: approved`, `revision: 1`, `children: [seeds-aa01..04]`
- **Validation-failure test**: missing `risks` (note: `risks` is optional — pick a *required* section like omit `context`); assert stderr is the partial-state diff JSON shape and `plans.jsonl` / `issues.jsonl` are unchanged
- **Resubmit-without-overwrite test**: second submit for the same seed errors and writes nothing
- Stdin input (`--plan -`) supported
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` green

## Files

- `src/commands/plan.ts` (edit)
- `src/commands/plan.test.ts` (edit — golden path, validation failure, resubmit rejection, stdin input)
- May add helpers in `src/id.ts` (plan id generation)

## Depends on

- Task 1 (types)
- Task 2 (store)
- Task 3 (template + AJV schema)
- Task 4 (command parent)
closeReason: sd plan submit: golden path, validation failure (no writes), resubmit rejection, stdin input, unknown seed all tested. step.blocks[idx] -> child.blockedBy id remap; parent gets plan_id + blockedBy=children

title: Phase 4 Task 1: requires_plan field on Issue + sd ready exclusion
description:
## What

Add an optional `requires_plan?: boolean` flag to the `Issue` interface, and teach `sd ready` to exclude seeds that have `requires_plan: true` AND either no plan or a plan whose status is `draft`. Once the seed's plan reaches `approved`, the seed becomes ready-eligible again (subject to the normal `blockedBy` rules from the children spawned by that plan).

This task lays the load-bearing invariant for the entire phase. Spec PLAN_SPEC.md:342 states:

> A child seed marked `requires_plan: true` is excluded from `sd ready` until its sub-plan reaches `approved`. This guarantees no implementation begins on un-planned epic branches.

The data-layer invariant: sub-plans can stack arbitrarily deep — only `sd plan show` is depth-limited (Task 3). The `requires_plan` flag is the gate that keeps un-planned epics out of the work queue, recursively.

Spec references: PLAN_SPEC.md:328-342 (Nested Plans), PLAN_SPEC.md:339-342 (bidirectional linking + ready-exclusion invariant).

## Why

Without this flag, `sd ready` would surface a spawned epic-typed child the moment its parent plan is approved, even though that child itself was declared as needing its own sub-plan. Implementation would begin on an un-planned branch — exactly what the planning workflow is designed to prevent.

This is the gate that propagates the plan-first discipline through arbitrarily deep decompositions.

## Acceptance

- `Issue` interface gains optional `requires_plan?: boolean` field; existing seeds without the field behave as before (treated as `false`).
- New test: create a seed with `requires_plan: true` (no plan attached). Assert it does NOT appear in `sd ready` output.
- Test: same seed, create a plan in `draft` status. Assert still hidden from `sd ready`.
- Test: transition plan to `approved`. Assert the seed now appears in `sd ready` (assuming no other blockers).
- Test: with the plan approved AND children spawned that block this seed via normal `blockedBy`, assert the seed remains hidden until children close — confirming the requires_plan check composes with the existing blocker check.
- `--json` output includes `requires_plan` when present.

## Files

- `src/types.ts` — add `requires_plan?: boolean` to `Issue` interface.
- `src/filter.ts` — extend the shared filter to honor `requires_plan` + plan-status check.
- `src/commands/ready.ts` — wire the new exclusion path. (May need to read `plans.jsonl` to check plan status; coordinate with the Phase 1 plan store.)
- Test files: extend `src/commands/ready.test.ts` (or create one) with the four cases above.
closeReason: implemented requires_plan field + sd ready exclusion; tests in src/commands/ready.test.ts

title: sd plan show <pl-id>
description:

title: Plan lifecycle auto-transitions (draft -> approved -> active -> done)
description:
## What

Wire automatic plan status transitions per PLAN_SPEC.md:111–127. The state machine:

```
draft  →  approved  →  active        →  done
              ↑             ↑               ↑
        sd plan submit  child→in_progress  all children closed
```

Transitions to wire:
1. `draft → approved`: Triggered by `sd plan submit` succeeding. Phase 1 already does this — verify the field is set and assert in tests.
2. `approved → active`: Triggered when ANY child seed transitions to `in_progress`. Hook into the `sd update --status in_progress` flow in `src/commands/update.ts`. Must look up `plan_id` on the seed and bump the plan's status if currently `approved`.
3. `active → done`: Triggered when ALL children of a plan are `closed`. Hook into `src/commands/close.ts` — after closing a seed with a `plan_id`, check the parent plan's children; if all closed, set plan status to `done` and stamp `updatedAt`.

Edge case: re-opening a closed child (transitioning from `closed` back to `open` or `in_progress`) when the plan is `done` must revert the plan to `active`. Hook this into `src/commands/update.ts` similarly.

All transitions atomic under the plans.jsonl lock.

## Why

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:109–127, plan status is derived state — managed by seeds, not the user. Without auto-transitions, plan status stays at `approved` forever, defeating queryability. The reverse transition (`done → active`) on child reopen is required by the acceptance criterion below.

## Acceptance

- `sd plan submit` sets plan status to `approved` (verify in test).
- Moving any child to `in_progress` flips the plan from `approved` to `active`.
- Closing the last open child flips plan status from `active` to `done` with `updatedAt` stamped.
- Re-opening a closed child of a `done` plan reverts plan status to `active`.
- Idempotent: closing an already-closed seed does not regress plan status; moving an already-`active` plan's child to `in_progress` is a no-op for plan status.
- Concurrent test: two children moved to `in_progress` simultaneously; only one final plan-status mutation observed (file lock works).
- Integration test: create plan with 2 children, walk through full lifecycle (draft → approved → active → done), then reopen one child and verify revert to `active`, close again and verify back to `done`.
- `bun test && bun run lint && bun run typecheck` clean.

## Files

- `src/commands/update.ts` — hook on status transitions
- `src/commands/close.ts` — hook on close
- `src/commands/plan-submit.ts` — verify draft→approved set
- `src/store.ts` — may need a helper `updatePlanStatus(planId, newStatus)` if not already present
- `src/commands/update.test.ts`, `src/commands/close.test.ts`, `src/commands/plan-lifecycle.test.ts` (NEW integration test)

## Spec references

- PLAN_SPEC.md:109–127 — plan lifecycle
- PLAN_SPEC.md:120–123 — transition triggers

## Notes

This task is INDEPENDENT of tasks 1–3 and 5; it can proceed in parallel. It touches plan store + status only, not templates or schemas.
closeReason: src/plan-lifecycle.ts: computeNextPlanStatus + applyPlanTransitions; update.ts/close.ts hook on status change under plans+issues lock; 17 tests covering pure rules, idempotency, lifecycle walk-through

title: sd plan submit --record-decision outbound ml record
description:
# `sd plan submit --record-decision` outbound write

Add an opt-in `--record-decision` flag to `sd plan submit` that calls
`ml record` after a successful submit, recording the chosen approach as a
mulch `decision` linked back to the plan.

## What

PLAN_SPEC.md:354-356: off by default. When `--record-decision` is passed,
on a successful submit, shell out:

```
ml record <inferred-domain> \
  --type decision \
  --rationale <approach-section-text> \
  --evidence-seeds <plan-id>
```

`<inferred-domain>` reuses the domain inference helper from the blocker
task. `<approach-section-text>` is the value of the plan's `approach`
section.

## Why

- Closes the loop: a chosen approach becomes durable mulch expertise linked
  to the plan that produced it.
- Off by default keeps seeds standalone (PLAN_SPEC.md:17, 354-356) and
  avoids surprising users who have `ml` installed but don't want every plan
  to mint a decision record.

## Acceptance

- `--record-decision` is a new flag on `sd plan submit`, default off.
- When the flag is set AND submit succeeds AND `ml` is on PATH AND a domain
  is inferred: `ml record` is invoked with the documented args.
- When `ml` is **not** on PATH (`Bun.which(\"ml\")` returns null): submit
  still succeeds; warn on stderr (`ml not found; skipping --record-decision`)
  but exit 0.
- When `ml record` exits non-zero: submit still succeeds atomically; warn
  on stderr but exit 0. The plan write must remain atomic — outbound write
  is best-effort.
- When no domain can be inferred: warn on stderr; submit still succeeds.
- **Integration test 1:** stub `ml` via a fake script on PATH that records
  invocation args. Submit with `--record-decision` -> asserts `ml record`
  was called with `<domain> --type decision --rationale <text>
  --evidence-seeds <plan-id>`.
- **Integration test 2:** stub `ml record` to exit non-zero. Submit still
  succeeds (exit 0), plan file written, stderr warns. Assert plan JSON is
  intact in `plans.jsonl`.
- **Integration test 3:** `ml` absent. Submit with `--record-decision`
  succeeds with stderr warning.

## Files

- Edit: `src/commands/plan.ts` — add `--record-decision` flag to the
  `submit` subcommand and post-success hook.
- Edit: `src/commands/plan.test.ts` (or new `src/commands/plan-submit.test.ts`)
  — integration tests for the three branches above.
- Reuses `src/plan-domain.ts` from the blocker task and `Bun.spawnSync`
  pattern from the prior_art task.

## Implementation notes

- Use `Bun.which(\"ml\")` to resolve — explicit, never assume PATH.
- Use `Bun.spawnSync` — matches existing seeds pattern.
- Graceful degradation is mandatory: the plan write must remain atomic. The
  outbound `ml record` call happens **after** the plan is fully persisted to
  `plans.jsonl` and child seeds are spawned. A failed `ml record` must not
  roll back any of that.
- Stderr warnings only — never throw, never exit non-zero from this path.

title: Phase 5: Outcomes, review, secondary templates, docs
description:
# Phase 5: Outcomes, review, secondary templates, docs

Rounds out the `sd plan` feature with the soft-review workflow, plan outcomes, the two remaining built-in templates (`bug`, `refactor`), and documentation. Lower priority than Phases 1-4 — these are enhancements, not the core spawn loop.

## Scope

Per PLAN_SPEC.md:
- Outcomes (lines 393-402): `sd plan outcome <pl-id> --result success|partial|failure [--note <text>]`
- Reviewer suggestion (lines 404-413): informational `reviewedBy` field + `sd plan review` command + show-hint
- Built-in `bug` template (line 268): adds `reproduction` and `root_cause` sections
- Built-in `refactor` template (line 269): adds `behavior_invariant` (must stay equal)
- Docs: README "Planning" section, CLAUDE.md updates, `sd prime` / `sd onboard` plan-aware context

## Cross-phase deps

Depends on Phase 2 (custom templates infrastructure) — `bug` and `refactor` flow through the same machinery as `feature`. Orchestrator wires Phase 2 -> Phase 5 at the feature level.

## Tasks

1. `sd plan outcome <pl-id> --result ... [--note ...]`
2. `sd plan review <pl-id> --by <name>` + `reviewedBy` field + "review suggested" hint
3. Built-in `bug` template
4. Built-in `refactor` template
5. Docs: README, CLAUDE.md, sd prime / sd onboard awareness

## Intra-phase deps

- Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4 are independent of each other.
- Task 5 (docs) depends on tasks 1-4 landing first.
- All tasks block this phase feature; this phase feature blocks parent epic seeds-8fdb.
